intent.design_goal: Design an ergonomically comfortable dining chair for residential use.

constraints.description: Primary material(s) for frame, seating, and upholstery.
Maximum weight the chair can support safely.
Preferred production technique (e.g., injection molding, CNC machining).
Desired design style (e.g., modern, vintage, ergonomic).

requirement_spec.design_goal: Design an ergonomically comfortable dining chair for residential use.

requirements.description: Design object type resolved as dining_chair
downstream_design_consequences: Initializes constraint graph even when numeric targets are absent

requirement_spec.completeness_rationale: All required engineering decisions resolved from prompt.

functional_analysis.primary_function: Design an ergonomically comfortable and structurally safe dining chair for residential use.

functions.name: Provide Seating Support
functions.description: Ensure the chair can support occupant weight and distribute load across the seat and frame.

functions.name: Load Distribution Analysis
functions.description: Analyze how occupant load is transmitted through seat, backrest, and frame to legs.

functions.name: Frame Design
functions.description: Define the geometry and joint configuration of the frame to resist bending and torsion under load.

functions.name: Ensure Ergonomic Comfort
functions.description: Create seat, backrest, and cushioning geometry that meets human factors guidelines for comfort.

functions.name: Seat Geometry Determination
functions.description: Determine seat height, depth and contour to align with anthropometric data.

functions.name: Cushioning Material Selection
functions.description: Select foam density, support, and comfort characteristics appropriate for residential use.

functions.name: Lumbar Support Design
functions.description: Develop the shape and embedment method for lumbar support to reduce lower‑back strain.

functions.name: Material Selection
functions.description: Choose primary materials for frame, seat, and upholstery based on strength, comfort, and aesthetics.

functions.name: Aesthetic Design Compliance
functions.description: Ensure the chair meets the chosen visual style (e.g., modern ergonomic) and finish requirements.

functions.name: Manufacturing Method Definition
functions.description: Determine applicable production techniques (e.g., CNC machining, injection molding) for each component.

functions.name: Assembly Integration
functions.description: Define sub‑assemblies and joining methods to construct the final chair product.

flows.description: Primary frame materials flow from material selection to frame design.
Cushioning materials flow from material selection to cushioning design.
Aesthetic design specifications inform assembly location and finish.
Manufacturing method constraints inform joint selection and assembly methods.
Load distribution data informs assembly strength and joint design.

required_assemblies.name: Frame Assembly
required_assemblies.function: Provide structural support and load transfer
required_assemblies.purpose: Create the main load‑bearing structure for the chair.

required_assemblies.name: Seat Assembly
required_assemblies.function: Deliver seating surface with proper comfort
required_assemblies.purpose: Combine seat geometry, cushioning, and upholstery into a single functional unit.

required_assemblies.name: Backrest Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Integrate backrest geometry and cushioning with lumbar support.

required_assemblies.name: Lumbar Support Sub‑assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Insert lumbar element into backrest or seat cushion.

required_assemblies.name: Leg Assembly
required_assemblies.function: Transfer load from seat to floor
required_assemblies.purpose: Provide stable, vertically oriented legs linked to the frame.

physics_warnings: No physics module registered for object_type='dining_chair'; ICE PhysicsEngine was not invoked.
